general_skills.title: Decompose the claim
general_skills.principle: Break the post into concrete factual claims before judging it.
general_skills.when_to_apply: At the start of every verification episode.

general_skills.title: Cross-check modalities
general_skills.principle: Compare post text, transcript, OCR, and frame descriptions for consistency.
general_skills.when_to_apply: When a post includes both text and video evidence.

general_skills.title: Prefer specific evidence
general_skills.principle: Base the verdict on explicit statements, dates, labels, and visible context rather than vibe-based guesses.

general_skills.title: Handle uncertainty honestly
general_skills.principle: If the provided evidence is insufficient, prefer an unverified verdict over overclaiming.
general_skills.when_to_apply: When evidence is incomplete or ambiguous.

manipulated_media.title: Look for impossible visual claims
manipulated_media.principle: Check whether text overlays or narration make absolute medical, political, or scientific claims unsupported by the frame context.
manipulated_media.when_to_apply: When dramatic certainty appears in captions or audio.

misleading_caption.title: Separate caption from evidence
misleading_caption.principle: Treat the caption as a claim to verify, not as evidence by itself.
misleading_caption.when_to_apply: When the post caption makes a strong assertion.

out_of_context.title: Verify time cues
out_of_context.principle: Search transcript, OCR, and frame descriptions for timestamps, archive labels, or signs that the video predates the claimed event.
out_of_context.when_to_apply: When the post says the clip is recent or tied to a current event.

source_credibility.title: Inspect source signals
source_credibility.principle: Use metadata such as author, platform context, and official language in transcript/OCR to assess whether the source looks authoritative.
source_credibility.when_to_apply: When authenticity depends on who is publishing the post.

temporal_inconsistency.title: Resolve timeline conflicts
temporal_inconsistency.principle: A mismatch between claimed date and visible/archive date is strong evidence of misinformation.
temporal_inconsistency.when_to_apply: When time references appear in the post or frames.

common_mistakes.description: Calling a post fake without citing concrete evidence.
common_mistakes.why_it_happens: The model reacts to suspicious tone instead of grounded details.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Quote specific transcript, OCR, or frame clues in the verdict evidence list.
Down-rank unsupported absolute claims unless the evidence package substantiates them.

common_mistakes.why_it_happens: Temporal clues are often small and easy to miss.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Inspect OCR and frame descriptions before issuing a verdict.
